title: Ensure that the maximumFileSizeMegabytes argument is set to 100

input_row.title: Ensure that the maximumFileSizeMegabytes argument is set to 100
input_row.description: Audit logs are rotated upon reaching a maximum size, which is 100 MB by default.
input_row.rationale: OpenShift automatically rotates the log files. Retaining old log files ensures that you would have sufficient log data available for carrying out any investigation or correlation. If you have set file size of 100 MB and the number of old log files to keep as 10, you would have approximately 1 GB of log data that you could potentially use for your analysis.
input_row.audit: OpenShift audit works at the API server level, logging all requests coming to the server. Configure via audit-log-maxsize . Run the following command: oc get configmap config -n openshift-kube-apiserver -ojson | jq -r '.data["config.yaml"]' | jq -r '.apiServerArguments["audit-log-maxsize"][]?' Verify that the audit-log-maxsize argument is set to 100
input_row.remediation: None. The audit-log-maxsize parameter is by default set to 100 and not supported to change. maximumFileSizeMegabytes: 100

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit control specifies an API check through the audit command provided ("oc get configmap config -n openshift-kube-apiserver -ojson | jq -r '.data[\"config.yaml\"]' | jq -r '.apiServerArguments[\"audit-log-maxsize\"][]?'"). This indicates that the audit property can be programmatically verified using existing APIs.
- IBM Cloud CLI SDK can be leveraged for this purpose, and commands can be issued to validate the configuration of the OpenShift cluster.
- The audit configuration explicitly talks about properties that are manageable through command-line tools, further supporting automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_openshift_audit_log_max_size_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the IBM Cloud CLI with the command: 
  ```
  oc get configmap config -n openshift-kube-apiserver -ojson | jq -r '.data["config.yaml"]' | jq -r '.apiServerArguments["audit-log-maxsize"][]?'
  ```
- The command retrieves the configuration map for the OpenShift API server and checks the `audit-log-maxsize` argument.
- Validate that the value retrieved equals `100`.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** The output value is `100`.
  - **Fail:** The output value is different than `100`, indicating misconfiguration or deviation from the benchmark. 

The implementation of this automated check will ensure compliance with the CIS benchmark specified for OpenShift regarding audit log maximum file size configuration.
